findings.description: Input guard wiring audit — VERIFIED CORRECT: sanitize_for_llm_prompt() is called on BOTH system_prompt and user_prompt in chat_flash() (lines 205-213) and chat_pro() (lines 239-248). Sanitization happens BEFORE the LLM call (_call_with_retry). Warnings are logged at WARNING level for both prompts. Sanitized values replace originals before being sent.

chat_with_integrity() at line 323-353: injects M1 integrity header + optional cash-reframing protocol into system_prompt, then delegates to chat_flash()/chat_pro() which apply input guard sanitization. The integrity header is hardcoded (no injection risk). Cash reframing substitution at lines 341-344 uses .replace() with str() conversion on capital — safe since template is hardcoded.

KeyRotator._remaining dict maps integer indices to Optional[int]. If a key is rotated out before a response is received, update_remaining (line 133) writes to the current key index which may have changed mid-flight. This is a quota-tracking accuracy issue, not a security issue. The asyncio.Lock at line 36 protects rotation but not the update_remaining call which is outside the lock.

RateLimitError handling at lines 286-296: one retry with key rotation on 429. No infinite retry loop. Shared-pool detection at lines 58-61, 179-185 logs warning once per session — helps operators detect misconfigured API keys.

notes: Input guard wiring is correctly implemented and verified. Sanitization happens before LLM calls on both prompts. Warnings are logged. The key rotation quota tracking race is a low-impact accuracy concern. No security vulnerabilities found in the modified code paths.
